title: Corrected crosswalk labels appear authoritative and correct
evidence: Top-five replay_score_mapping has 150 unique team-area-case rows; official scenario codes A1-A7/B1-B4/C1-C4 each appear 10 times; spot checks match raw SQL case-name sequences.
recommendation: Keep corrected labels; cite official score CSV plus raw SQL case names.

title: SQL provenance pointers are not exact-session for most rows
evidence: Only 30/150 scenario_map_sql_line pointers hit the exact mapped session row; 120/150 point to another session with the same area/case-name sequence.
impact: Auditability defect but no label-changing mismatch found.
recommendation: Repair line pointers or clarify that pointers are area/case-name exemplars, not exact session evidence.

title: A1 and safety identity corrected
evidence: Official A1 rows total 10; only T15 and T20 Beijing case 2333 have safety=0, coordination=0, comprehensive=0; collision-free is safety>=100.

title: Primary flip comes from legitimate label/inclusion change
evidence: Corrected primary N=53 equals old N=48 plus five old-label A1 rows that are official A7; no old-primary cells dropped.
impact: Supports flip legitimacy as an error-fix consequence.
recommendation: Document N change and avoid implying same-sample robustness.

title: In-memory LOSO regeneration is an error-fix but needs formalization
evidence: Primary LOTO did not depend on LOSO regeneration; corrected LOSO/LOFO used official labels because frozen fold_contract.csv has the old erroneous A1-C5 grid.
impact: No primary-fold contract blocker found, but secondary/boundary claims need an amended contract.
recommendation: Create an amended corrected fold contract before final release.

title: Future-leaky cache missingness did not drive the primary flip
evidence: Future-leaky cache covers 48/53 corrected primary cells and is excluded from primary robustness claims.
impact: Non-primary diagnostic remains incomplete and positive.
recommendation: Do not use future-leaky as robustness evidence; recompute only if retaining as a diagnostic.

title: State-dependence multiple comparisons do not promote local claims
evidence: Interpretable state strata n=90, no q<=0.10, minimum q=0.628317.
impact: No state-stratum overclaim found beyond wording caution.
recommendation: Keep state-dependence as exploratory boundary mapping.

title: IPV is not a simple single kinematic recoding but criterion limits remain
evidence: Largest corrected-primary IPV-baseline absolute Spearman was about 0.31; coordination correlated with comprehensive score rho=0.420 and heading-difference AUC rho=-0.522.
impact: Interpretation boundary, not a computation blocker.
recommendation: Use official generated coordination score wording, not human social-ground-truth wording.

title: Reader metadata is stale
evidence: 00_entry, 90_report, README, and execution_status still describe initialized/pending/no-statistics status.
recommendation: Update only after blockers are resolved and write scope permits.
